text: Web security is a critical concern for any developer. One essential practice is using HTTPS to encrypt data sent between a user's browser and the server, protecting it from eavesdroppers. Strong authentication methods, like requiring complex passwords or two-factor authentication, help verify user identities. Equally important is validating all user input; never trust data from forms or URLs, as malicious code can lead to common vulnerabilities, such as SQL injection or cross-site scripting (XSS). By blocking unexpected characters and using parameterized queries, developers can prevent attackers from manipulating databases or injecting harmful scripts. Implementing these layers of defense makes websites significantly more secure against threats.
